entries.role: project proponent / EIS preparer
entries.summary_quote: The proposed action is the construction and operation of a 2-unit nuclear plant in Tishomingo County, Mississippi.

mentions.stance_basis: TVA prepared the EIS and proposes construction and operation of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact text 'The proposed action is the construction and operation of a 2-unit nuclear plant in Tishomingo County, Mississippi.' appears verbatim on the cited page.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stance (paraphrased)
rubric_results.note: The cited page clearly states the EIS was 'prepared by the Tennessee Valley Authority,' establishing TVA as the proponent/preparer of this proposed action.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: TVA is the preparer and proponent of the nuclear plant construction; 'in_favor' is appropriate.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'Tennessee Valley Authority' is the official name of the agency.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The page explicitly states the environmental statement was 'prepared by the Tennessee Valley Authority,' confirming both project proponent and EIS preparer roles.

critic.notes: All checks pass. The summary quote is verbatim, TVA is clearly identified as the EIS preparer and project proponent, the stance of 'in_favor' is well-supported, and the entity name and role are accurate per the cited text.

entries.role: project applicant / commenter on EPA comments
entries.summary_quote: Therefore, although TVA considered both locations suitable for the proposed facility, based on an overall assessment of the engineering, environmental, and economic characteristics of both candidate sites TVA selected the Yellow Creek site as the preferred location for the proposed 2-unit plant.

mentions.quote: TVA's conclusion is that restructuring of rates within the requirements of the TVA Act cannot be shown to be a reliable means of reducing the peak demand for power and is thus not a feasible alternative to the construction of new generating capacity.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA concludes rate restructuring is not a feasible alternative, supporting need for new generating capacity (the plant)

mentions.quote: TVA performs most of the work with its own forces, thus providing closer control over construction operations which could result in adverse environmental impacts.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA described as actively managing and conducting construction of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant

mentions.quote: TVA believes that effluent monitoring provides more effective data than instream monitoring to evaluate the potential for impact of plant discharges on water quality.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA asserts its own monitoring approach and defends the adequacy of the plant's environmental program

mentions.quote: The addition of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant to the TVA system will enable TVA to continue to carry out its responsibility to provide an ample supply of electricity for the TVA region.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA frames the plant as necessary to fulfill its regional electricity mandate

mentions.quote: Therefore, although TVA considered both locations suitable for the proposed facility, based on an overall assessment of the engineering, environmental, and economic characteristics of both candidate sites TVA selected the Yellow Creek site as the preferred location for the proposed 2-unit plant.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA selected Yellow Creek site as preferred location for the nuclear plant

mentions.quote: TVA concurs with the EPA comment on the need to have the FES reflect the same flow rates as those in the NPDES permit application and in the draft NPDES permit.
mentions.stance_basis: TVA repeatedly concurs with EPA comments and defends the adequacy of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant EIS and project
mentions.role_as_written: project applicant / commenter on EPA comments

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact quote 'Therefore, although TVA considered both locations suitable for the proposed facility, based on an overall assessment of the engineering, environmental, and economic characteristics of both candidate sites TVA selected the Yellow Creek site as the preferred location for the proposed 2-unit plant.' does not appear verbatim in the provided page excerpts. The pages discuss site selection and candidate sites but do not contain this exact sentence.

rubric_results.check: Attribution links TVA to stated stance
rubric_results.note: The cited pages clearly discuss TVA's site selection process, including evaluation of Yellow Creek and Saltillo sites, and TVA's role as project applicant is evident throughout.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: TVA is clearly in favor of the Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant project based on the content of the cited pages.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: TVA (Tennessee Valley Authority) is an official agency name and is well-formed.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: TVA is clearly the project applicant and is shown commenting on EPA comments in the cited pages (pages 337 area), supporting the dual role description.

critic.notes: The summary quote is marked as verified but does not appear verbatim in the provided page excerpts. While the substance of the quote (TVA selecting Yellow Creek as preferred site after evaluating both candidate sites) is consistent with the content on page 289 discussing site selection, the exact sentence is not present in the pulled text. The quote may exist on one of the cited pages not fully reproduced here, but as presented it fails the verbatim check. The extractor should verify the exact page location of this quote and confirm verbatim presence.

entries.role: federal nuclear regulator
entries.summary_quote: The NRC has performed a detailed study to assess these risks more quantitatively.
